summary: This source is the ACM press release announcing Richard S. Sutton and Andrew G. Barto as the recipients of the 2024 A.M. Turing Award. It provides a high-level historical overview of their foundational contributions to reinforcement learning, but as a third-party biographical citation, it does not contain Sutton's direct quotes, personal heuristics, or detailed thought processes.

mental_models.name: Agent-Reward Model
mental_models.description: Viewing artificial intelligence through the lens of an 'agent' that perceives and acts within a stochastic environment. The agent's goal is to maximize its long-term cumulative reward based on a signal that denotes the quality of its behavior, even when the environment and the rewards are initially unknown.
mental_models.example: This model has been applied to a vast range of problems, including robot motor skill learning (like solving a Rubik's Cube), network congestion control, and training large language models via reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF).
